summary: Added 9 new keys (readiness.anchor.low/okay/high/rough/steady/good/poor/fine/great) across all 19 locales with LLM-drafted translations. Wired 6 call sites: 3 TodayView check-in readinessRow anchors (Energy/Mood/Sleep), 1 TodayView PreviousDay TrainingReadinessScaleRow, 2 TrainView readiness disclosures (inline + session editor). All anchor tuples now use String(localized:) so the inner Text(anchors.0/1/2) displays the already-localized string verbatim. The TrainingReadinessScaleRow / readinessRow body code was NOT modified — the existing Text(varString) display path correctly shows the resolved localized string. Localization-parity 19/339/13.
validations_passed: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-anchors-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
missing_proof_levels: running-app-smoke per locale (Today check-in section + Train readiness disclosure)

contract_status_changes.before: TodayView Energy / Mood / Sleep rows and TodayView PreviousDay + TrainView (2 places) passed raw English tuples like ("Low", "Okay", "High") to readinessRow / TrainingReadinessScaleRow. The inner Text(anchors.0) displayed the raw string verbatim, so German VoiceOver and German visible UI both showed English Low/Okay/High under the scale circles.
contract_status_changes.after: Each anchor tuple is built from String(localized: "readiness.anchor.<word>") at the call site. The inner display code is unchanged. 9 new keys exist across all 19 locales. German users now see Niedrig/Okay/Hoch / Mies/Stabil/Gut / Schlecht/In Ordnung/Großartig under the scale circles.

residual_risks: LLM-drafted translations are unverified by a native speaker. Subtle wording (e.g., German Mies for Rough is conversational and might not match Owlory voice) may need adjustment.

The same key (readiness.anchor.low/okay/high) is reused for Energy intensity (Today check-in) AND Training readiness (Train session). Some languages may prefer distinct translations for the two contexts — currently shared.
Rough/Steady/Good and Poor/Fine/Great are Mood- and Sleep-specific; reusing them in other contexts would need consideration.

The TrainingReadinessScaleRow / readinessRow internal `Text(anchors.X)` path was NOT modified. It works because the input strings are now already-localized via String(localized:) at the call site. If anyone passes a non-localized String tuple in the future, it will silently bypass localization again.

Per-locale review return files snapshot 372 entries from 2026-05-16; the 9 new keys are NOT reflected there. Dashboard / LQA / German-packet now show stale counts. Housekeeping refresh can be re-run with python3 Tools/localization-return-files-refresh.py --apply.
Native review remains outstanding for every locale.
recommended_next_reason: Next priority Features-located slice (in-place wrap). Pairs naturally with the anchors slice since both are user-visible Today copy. Then the Domain-located slices (readiness-rules / pattern-nudge-rules / calibration-rules / focus-suggestion-reason / continue-row-subtitle) can be done one at a time using the structural-refactor approach.
